description: Push 2 of the retention lifecycle: a bad partial fix with added bugs, as an open-PR re-review snapshot. The purge and the unbounded prune are properly fixed; the off-by-one is 'fixed' to a different off-by-one (offset MAX+1) so its thread must be KEPT; the test fix is deferred in a reply so its thread must be KEPT; and the new dedup feature plants a fresh blocking defect (a lowercased 8-char prefix key silently drops distinct notes) plus an untested dedup window.

evidence_trace: src/notes/retention.ts: saveNote returns without put when the key is seen
finding.failure_scenario: A user saves two distinct notes whose first 8 characters match case-insensitively (e.g. two 'reminder: ...' notes); dedupKey collides, the second note is treated as a duplicate, and it is silently never stored - data loss with no error.
finding.model_authored_prose: Dedup keys on a lowercased 8-character prefix, so distinct notes sharing a prefix collide and the later one is silently dropped. Compare full content (or a real hash), not a prefix.

evidence_trace: src/notes/retention.test.ts: no test constructs a note older than the window
finding.failure_scenario: A regression that drops the createdAt filter (deduping against all history) or inverts its comparison would pass CI: no test saves a duplicate outside the 30-day window.
finding.model_authored_prose: The new 30-day dedup window has no test on either side of the boundary; a regression in the window filter would pass CI silently.

prContext.title: notes: cap stored notes per user and delete them with user data
prContext.description: Adds a per-user retention cap enforced on save, and wires note deletion into account deletion. Push 2: review fixes plus save-time dedup.
prContext.author: dev-notes

priorThreads.body: **issue (blocking):** Account deletion removes at most one note. `query("Note", {userId})` uses the facade's default `pageSize` of 1 (src/notes/db.ts), so `deleteMulti` receives a single id and every other note survives the purge.
priorThreads.authorReply: Good catch - purge now pages through every batch of 100 until the query returns nothing.

priorThreads.body: **issue (blocking):** Off-by-one - with newest-first ordering, `offset: MAX_NOTES_PER_USER - 1` keeps one note fewer than the cap. Use `offset: MAX_NOTES_PER_USER`.
**issue (blocking):** The prune query loads the entire stale result set at once (`pageSize: "all"`). Delete in bounded batches so a large backlog cannot balloon a save.
**todo (blocking):** The prune test saves 2 notes against a cap of 200 and asserts only that the promise resolves; it can never exercise the cap. Save past the cap and assert the retained count.
priorThreads.authorReply: Will tighten the test in a follow-up.
